sections.paragraph:
(I) (from the root דָּלָה), pr. something hanging, swinging, hence the leaf of a door as being hung up, and swinging both ways. Once used metaph. the door of the lips, for mouth, Psa 141:3 (compare Mic 7:5, and πύλαι στόματος, Eurip. Hippol. 882). By far more frequent is fem. דֶּלֶת a door, which see.

(II.) plur. דַּלִּיםִ, fem. דַּלֹּות feeble (from the root דָּלַל) weak, powerless. 2Sa 3:1, “David became continually stronger וּבֵית שָׁאוּל הֹלְכִים וְדַלִּים and the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker;” specially-(a) lean, Gen 41:19 2Sa 13:4, מַדּוּעַ אַתָּה כָּכָה דַּל “why art thou so lean?”-(b) weak, low, ignoble. Often in plur. דַּלִּים Exo 23:3 Levit. 14:21 19:15 1Sa 2:8 Rth 3:10 Psa 41:2 72:13 Pro 10:15 14:31 19:4 Isa 14:30 25:4 26:6 .
